results.title: AgentManager: Never miss a Claude Code session waiting ...
results.content:
Hi Product Hunt! 👋 I'm a solo developer from Japan, and like many of you I run several Claude Code sessions in parallel. I kept doing the same dumb thing: kick off a session, switch to something else, and realize ten minutes later that Claude had been sitting there waiting for a single "yes" the whole time. AgentManager is a native macOS app that fixes exactly that: 🐈 A floating window shows every session's state — running, waiting, done ⚡ It surfaces the moment a session needs your input, and hides when all is clear 🖱️ Click a row to jump straight to the right terminal (iTerm2 pane, Terminal tab, VS Code / Cursor window…) 🔔 Cat-meow alerts instead of Notification Center banners (mutable, promise) 🎨 Sessions live as pixel-art cats in a little room — day and night change with your clock. [...] Subscribe

umechanhika

Custom Browsing History [...] in a little room — day and night change with your clock. There's a Simple mode if cats aren't your thing. A few things that matter to me: • Native Swift, ~2.5 MB download, notarized by Apple • Your session data (prompts, paths, names) never leaves your Mac • 7-day free trial with no credit card. Then $4.99/mo or $48/yr. I'd love your feedback — especially which terminals or IDEs you'd like better jump support for. I'll be here all day answering questions!

What if you could manage all your projects and CLI agents in one place? I got this idea while looking at Antigravity's agent manager. And watching all these AI tools constantly updating, I became convinced that I shouldn't be locked into any single AI or tool.

Amazing, finally someone did this. I was looking for something like this for a while. How does it manage restarts? Let's say I run some "export" commands, then start long running process. Then after restart, if I open CLI Manager again - what will I see? will it restart my processes? Will it preserve "env"? Will "history" be shared between tabs?

results.title: SmythOS: SmythOS: The open source operating system for AI agents | Product Hunt
results.content:
Launched on December 11th, 2024

4.8

Based on 13 reviews

Review SmythOS?

Reviewers say SmythOS is strongest when it makes agent building visual, fast, and practical: users repeatedly praise the drag-and-drop interface, broad integrations, templates, and Agent Weaver’s ability to turn a short prompt into a working agent. They describe real workflow gains, especially for content, social media, sales, and multi-agent setups that mix models, tools, code, and APIs. The main caveat is onboarding: some say the platform feels complex at first and needs clearer getting-started guidance, plus wider support for more API providers.

 10 views

•2 reviews

Arguably the best and most intuitive visual AI agent creation interfaces - even for a non-techinal person like me. Sort of like Zapier for AI, but x1000. And the chat-to-create-agent feature is basically magic. But I find the separate Workspaces feature most useful. It helps me create separate workspaces for my teams and clients, and makes project management a breeze. Easily the best investment I've made as a marketing consultant. Excited to see what the team creates next!
